page: Microsoft Azure E16-8ads_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with a base frequency of 3.525 GHz. The instance is configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. Memory capacity is 128.0 GB, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is bundled, providing 614 GB of temporary disk space, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% at 2702.98. Multi-core performance and general computational tasks, such as software compilation and image processing, fall consistently within the average range. Memory read bandwidth is average, reaching 514.43 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 39.67 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, a notable tradeoff is observed in memory write bandwidth for large blocks, which is weak at 22.49 GB/sec, placing it in the bottom 25%. Large-model LLM inference is also a bottleneck, with prompt processing for a medium 7B model registering as weak at 17.35 tokens/sec, though text generation for the same model remains average at 9.45 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E16-8ads_v5 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute scaling. The inclusion of 614 GB of local storage reduces the immediate need for external storage attachments for temporary data. This profile is applicable for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data buffering, and moderate-scale web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-8ads_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local storage. The instance exhibits strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core benchmarks and memory read bandwidth remain in the average tier. A key tradeoff is its weak large-block memory write bandwidth and limited performance in medium-scale LLM prompt processing. Qualitatively, its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and bundled local storage make it cost-effective for memory-intensive applications that do not demand extensive parallel processing. It is appropriate for database operations, in-memory caching, and general enterprise workloads requiring substantial memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8ads_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage, designed for database and in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8ads_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage.
tagline: An AMD-powered, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-core ratio and integrated local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 3.525 GHz clock speed
128 GB of system memory providing a high 16:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
614 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU performance combined with average multi-core capabilities
Weak large-block memory write bandwidth and medium-model LLM prompt processing
Fits database operations, in-memory caching, and memory-bound enterprise applications
